copy.title: Smith leads Schwab. SPY treading water. Memorial preview incoming.
copy.marketsTake:
**Markets**

Microsoft's 5.4% rip on Friday was real—Satya Nadella's Copilot bet is finally printing money in enterprise deals, and Wall Street's repricing the AI narrative away from chip scarcity toward software that actually moves the needle. The gap between NVDA and MSFT tells you where the market thinks the real margin is, and it's getting harder to ignore that Jensen Huang's hardware play looks like a commodity business compared to Nadella's rent-seeking moat.

Watch next week for whether Microsoft's momentum holds or if this was a one-day relief pump. If rate cuts are coming (and the bond market's pricing them in), software should stay hot—TSLA and NVDA got rotated out Friday because their valuations can't survive lower discount rates, so you're basically seeing the market vote that mega-cap software is recession-proof and rate-insensitive
copy.golfNote: Jordan Smith's -10 lead at Schwab means he's playing the par-5s like a man with something to prove.
copy.sharpTake:
# Sharp Take

The weekend is yours if you want it. Jordan Smith's leading the Schwab Challenge at ten-under—he's hunting his first win on tour and won't fold. The market barely moved Friday (SPY up 0.2%), which means Monday's the real test; nothing to do here except let your positions breathe. On the culture front: entertainers bailing on Trump's State Fair and a Backrooms horror flick doing $9M in previews tells you exactly where attention is right now. None of it matters to your Saturday.

Clear your afternoon. Tell whoever asks you're unavailable until sundown.
